title: reset all data - also suggest info to store the generate code if needed in fu...
body:
## ✨ Feature Request

reset all data - also suggest info to store the generate code if needed in future.

---

**Category:** `enhancement` `content`
**Source:** Telegram Bot
**Status:** Pending implementation

> _This issue was created automatically via the BabyBloom Telegram Bot. No private baby data is included._
enhanced_description:
User wants a "Reset All Data" feature that clears all stored baby data, with a suggestion to save/store the generated share code before resetting so they can recover or re-share in the future.
- Affected area: Settings / Data management / IndexedDB storage
- Expected: A reset option that warns the user, suggests saving their share code, then clears all data
- Current: No reset all data feature exists
- Edge cases: Confirming destructive action, backing up share code first, handling shared profiles
- Acceptance criteria: Settings has a reset option with confirmation dialog that reminds user to save their share code, then clears all local data and returns to welcome screen
implementation_notes:
Files changed: src/features/settings/Settings.tsx
- Added info banner with shield icon in Reset section advising users to save their sync code before resetting
- Updated confirm dialog to ask "Have you saved your sync code?"
- Points users to the Sync / Share Data button above

title: if mother has been not using the app, the alert might say that they have not ...
body:
## 🐛 Bug Report

if mother has been not using the app, the alert might say that they have not done some activity for days, months or year. Similarly after age 2, tracking is not needed. So notify  right messages instead.

---

**Category:** `bug`
**Source:** Telegram Bot
**Status:** Pending implementation

> _This issue was created automatically via the BabyBloom Telegram Bot. No private baby data is included._
enhanced_description:
When a mother has not used the app for a long time, alerts may incorrectly say activities have not been done for days/months/years, which is alarming and unhelpful. Also after age 2, tracking alerts are not needed. The app should send contextually appropriate messages instead of raw time-since-last-activity alerts.
- Affected area: Alert/notification system / Time-based reminders
- Expected: If the user has been inactive for extended periods, show a gentle "welcome back" instead of alarming gap alerts. After age 2, suppress or modify tracking alerts.
- Current: Alerts show literal time gaps (e.g., "no feeding for 30 days") which is incorrect/alarming
- Edge cases: Varying inactivity durations, baby age transitions, re-engagement after long breaks
- Acceptance criteria: Alerts are contextually appropriate — long gaps show welcome-back messages, post-2yr alerts are suppressed or adapted
implementation_notes:
Files changed: src/features/insights/useDynamicRedFlags.ts, src/tabs/HomeTab.tsx
- Post-2yr (age >= 24 months): all tracking alerts suppressed in both red flags and quick-log warnings
- Long inactivity (3+ days): shows gentle "Welcome back" message instead of alarming gap alerts
- Both systems check for inactivity and short-circuit before computing individual warnings

title: when baby reaches 2 years of age, when they open the app for the first time s...
body:
## ✨ Feature Request

when baby reaches 2 years of age, when they open the app for the first time show a courasel similar to welcome screen on all the activities that they have done. Only important ones. Make them feel good. at the end, suggest that, you can still use the app, but there will not be any alert. The caurasel is shown only once but they can open it again from the hero widget. Post 2 years age data should not come in this courasel.

---

**Category:** `enhancement` `ui`
**Source:** Telegram Bot
**Status:** Pending implementation

> _This issue was created automatically via the BabyBloom Telegram Bot. No private baby data is included._
enhanced_description:
When baby reaches 2 years old, show a celebratory carousel (similar to the welcome screen) on first app open highlighting important milestone activities. Make parents feel good about the journey. At the end, suggest they can still use the app but alerts will stop. The carousel is shown only once but can be reopened from the hero widget. Post-2yr data should NOT appear in this carousel.
- Affected area: Hero widget / Welcome/milestone carousel / Age-based triggers
- Expected: One-time celebratory carousel at 2yr milestone showing key activities, with option to reopen from hero widget
- Current: No age-based milestone celebration exists
- Edge cases: Baby already past 2yrs on first install, carousel content when minimal data exists, multiple babies
- Acceptance criteria: Carousel appears once when baby turns 2, shows pre-2yr highlights only, has a warm congratulatory tone, dismissible, re-accessible from hero widget, and ends with a note about continued usage without alerts
implementation_notes:
Files changed: src/components/onboarding/MilestoneCarousel.tsx (new), src/tabs/HomeTab.tsx
- New MilestoneCarousel component with celebratory slides highlighting pre-2yr journey stats
- Auto-shows once when baby reaches 24 months (tracked via IndexedDB milestone2yrSeen flag)
- Re-accessible via "Relive the journey" button on hero widget when age >= 24 months
- Slides: intro, feeding stats, diaper count, sleep sessions, tummy time, baths, closing with milestones summary
- Post-2yr data excluded from carousel per requirements

title: on the hero widget for relevant activity doesn’t have last activity time
body:
## ✨ Feature Request

on the hero widget for relevant activity doesn’t have last activity time. Only feed has. Also include dirty and wet time in the diaper.

---

**Category:** `enhancement` `feeding` `diaper`
**Source:** Telegram Bot
**Status:** Pending implementation

> _This issue was created automatically via the BabyBloom Telegram Bot. No private baby data is included._
enhanced_description:
The hero widget on the home screen shows last activity time only for feeds. The user wants similar last-activity timestamps for diapers (wet + dirty separate) and sleep.
- Affected area: HomeTab hero widget — the today's quick stats section
- Expected: Each stat card (feeds, diapers, sleep) shows the last relevant activity time below the count
- Current: Only the feed stat card shows the last feed time and type
- Edge cases: No activity logged yet today, very old last activity
- Acceptance criteria: Diaper stat shows last diaper time with wet/dirty breakdown; sleep stat shows last sleep entry time
implementation_notes:
Files changed: src/tabs/HomeTab.tsx
- Added lastDiaperToday/lastDiaperLabel and lastSleepToday/lastSleepLabel computations
- Diaper stat card now shows last diaper type (Wet/Dirty/Both) and time
- Sleep stat card now shows last sleep type (Nap/Night) and time
- Matches existing feed card pattern with same styling

title: I don’t think static reminder (notifications) makes sense, it should be on th...
body:
## ✨ Feature Request

I don’t think static reminder (notifications) makes sense, it should be on the basis of age of the baby. Limit it to feed only. In the setting just have option to enable / disable reminder notifications

---

**Category:** `enhancement` `feeding`
**Source:** Telegram Bot
**Status:** Pending implementation

> _This issue was created automatically via the BabyBloom Telegram Bot. No private baby data is included._
enhanced_description:
The feeding reminder notification uses a static interval (2h, 2.5h, 3h, 4h). The user wants age-based smart reminders that automatically adjust to the baby's age. Also simplify settings to just enable/disable.
- Affected area: Settings > Feeding Reminders section + reminder logic in App.tsx
- Expected: Reminders auto-calculate interval based on baby age (newborn: 2-3h, 3-6mo: 3-4h, 6-12mo: 4-5h, 12mo+: less frequent). Settings simplified to just a toggle.
- Current: User manually picks from 2h, 2.5h, 3h, 4h intervals
- Edge cases: Age not set, transitioning between age brackets, night vs day timing
- Acceptance criteria: Reminder interval auto-adjusts based on age; Settings shows only enable/disable toggle with current interval displayed as info text
implementation_notes:
Files changed: src/App.tsx, src/tabs/HomeTab.tsx, src/features/settings/Settings.tsx
- Replaced static feedInterval with smartFeedInterval computed from baby age
- Newborn 2h, 1-3mo 2.5h, 3-6mo 3h, 6-9mo 3.5h, 9-12mo 4h, 12mo+ 5h
- Simplified Settings: removed interval picker pills, replaced with enable/disable toggle + info text
- Both App.tsx notification logic and HomeTab display use the same smart interval
- Backward compatible: old feedInterval in storage is ignored in favor of age-based calculation
